rationale: Ground truth's substantive concern (dsocek's note about PEFT naming-conflict workarounds and driving the mapper from _packed_modules_mapping) appears to reference code removed before the final diff (per tthakkal's reply), so neither candidate could realistically recall it; both instead independently converge on the adjacent, thematically-related critique that default_components duplicates _dit_modules/registry.py knowledge. Both are well-grounded with specific file:line citations that partially cross-validate (pipeline_sdxl.py:46, registry.py:427). Y edges out slightly with one extra grounded, actionable finding (missing SDXL integration-test coverage, verified via a zero-hit grep) and a second concrete alternative design (pipeline-declared _lora_components), while X's review is cleaner and its text-encoder no-op catch is sharp but doesn't offset Y's marginally broader coverage.
